phases.name: Manual release workflow (US-01)
phases.description: Version-alignment tooling, updated PUBLISHING.md release procedure, and README install instructions pointing to GitHub Releases. No CI automation yet — enables a human-driven release with verified version consistency.
deliverables: PUBLISHING.md (gh release steps + plugin.json checklist item)

steps.name: Write check-version.js script
steps.description: Create scripts/check-version.js as an ESM module using Node.js built-ins only. Reads version from package.json (source of truth), plugin.json (plugins/coach-buddy/.claude-plugin/plugin.json), SKILL.md frontmatter (skills/coach-buddy/SKILL.md — warn only if absent, do not exit non-zero), and the most recent CHANGELOG.md heading (## vX.Y or ## vX.Y.Z). Compares each source to the package.json version. Exits 0 on full alignment. Exits non-zero on any mismatch, printing the name of the offending file.
steps.criteria: npm run check:version exists and runs the script; script exits 0 when all sources match; script exits non-zero and prints the offending filename when any mandatory source mismatches package.json; missing SKILL.md version field produces a warning but exits 0; missing CHANGELOG.md heading exits non-zero
quality_gates: No third-party dependencies — Node.js built-ins only (fs, path, url, process)
Script is ESM (import/export) consistent with package.json type:module
Exit code is non-zero (process.exit(1)) on any mandatory mismatch
Offending filename printed to stderr on mismatch
SKILL.md absence/missing-version-field is a warning (stderr) not an error
implementation_notes: SKILL.md version is in YAML frontmatter: metadata.version field. Parse between --- delimiters with a regex; do not introduce a YAML library.
CHANGELOG.md heading pattern: /^## v(\d+\.\d+(?:\.\d+)?)/m — match first occurrence only.
plugin.json path: plugins/coach-buddy/.claude-plugin/plugin.json — confirm key name by inspecting file at implementation time.
Use import.meta.url + fileURLToPath to resolve paths relative to the script, not cwd.
Keep all logic in a single exported function (checkVersions(rootDir)) so unit tests can call it directly without spawning a subprocess.

steps.name: Register check:version npm script
steps.description: Add 'check:version': 'node scripts/check-version.js' to the scripts block in package.json so the script is runnable via npm run check:version.
steps.criteria: npm run check:version runs scripts/check-version.js without error when all versions are aligned at 1.7.0
quality_gates: Script key name is exactly check:version (colon separator, consistent with existing test/build scripts)
implementation_notes: One-line change to the scripts block. No other modifications to package.json.

steps.name: Update PUBLISHING.md with release procedure
steps.description: Add GitHub Releases distribution procedure to the Plugin build (CoWork) section of PUBLISHING.md. Include: npm run check:version before build:plugin; the gh release create command after build:plugin; plugin.json in the version-alignment checklist.
steps.criteria: PUBLISHING.md includes npm run check:version in the pre-build step; PUBLISHING.md includes 'gh release create v${VERSION} coach-buddy.plugin --title "v${VERSION}" --notes "See CHANGELOG.md"' in the release procedure; plugin.json appears in the version-alignment checklist alongside package.json and SKILL.md
quality_gates: No existing checklist items removed or reworded
gh release create command is copy-pasteable (no broken line wraps)
CHANGELOG.md release notes guidance is present alongside the gh command
implementation_notes: Insert check:version step in the Plugin build section between the checklist and the 'npm run build:plugin' command.
Add a new 'Distribute via GitHub Releases' subsection after the existing CoWork upload instructions.
The VERSION variable in the gh command should match the pattern used in PUBLISHING.md; document that it must equal package.json version.

steps.name: Update README CoWork install section
steps.description: Replace the clone-and-build install instructions in the README CoWork section with a direct GitHub Releases download URL. Users should be directed to download the latest coach-buddy.plugin from https://github.com/danjmfox-ovo/coach-buddy/releases/latest.
steps.criteria: README CoWork install section references the GitHub Releases download URL and does not mention clone-and-build as the primary path
quality_gates: URL is accurate and points to the correct repo releases page
Clone-and-build instructions are either removed or demoted to a developer/contributing note
implementation_notes: Keep the CoWork upload steps (Settings > Plugins > Upload plugin > Install) — only the artifact source changes.
If the README currently has no CoWork section, add one. If it has a combined section, split or annotate clearly.

phases.name: CI automation (US-02)
phases.description: GitHub Actions workflow that triggers on version tags and workflow_dispatch, runs check:version and build:plugin, then creates a GitHub Release with coach-buddy.plugin attached. No additional secrets beyond GITHUB_TOKEN. Failed workflow must not leave a partial or draft release.

steps.name: Create GitHub Actions release workflow
steps.description: Create .github/workflows/release.yml. Triggers: push to tags matching v*, workflow_dispatch. Steps in order: checkout (full history not needed), setup Node (version from engines.node, i.e. >=18 — use 22), run npm run check:version (failure stops workflow), run npm run build:plugin with a lightweight JSON schema check replacing claude plugin validate (see implementation notes), create GitHub Release with softprops/action-gh-release or gh CLI attaching coach-buddy.plugin using GITHUB_TOKEN. No draft release step — release is created atomically on success only.
steps.criteria: Workflow file exists at .github/workflows/release.yml triggering on push to v* tags and workflow_dispatch; check:version runs as first substantive step and failure stops the workflow; build:plugin runs using lightweight JSON schema check (not claude plugin validate) and failure stops the workflow; GitHub Release is created with coach-buddy.plugin attached using GITHUB_TOKEN on success only; no partial or draft release is left on workflow failure
quality_gates: Workflow uses actions/checkout@v4 and actions/setup-node@v4 (pinned major versions)
GITHUB_TOKEN permissions scoped to contents: write only (principle of least privilege)
build:plugin step uses the lightweight JSON schema check (node -e inline) not claude plugin validate — the claude CLI is not available in CI
Release creation step is the final step — no step follows it that could leave state on failure
workflow_dispatch trigger has no required inputs (manual trigger must work with zero configuration)
implementation_notes: claude plugin validate is not available in CI — the build:plugin script in package.json calls it. For CI, override or replace: run npm run build:plugin after patching the script, OR inline the JSON schema check (node -e 'const p=JSON.parse(...); if(!p.skills) process.exit(1)') as a separate workflow step and call the zip steps directly. Coordinate with the build:plugin script owner — the simplest fix is a separate build:plugin:ci script that skips the claude validate call.
Use softprops/action-gh-release@v2 for release creation — it handles tag name extraction from GITHUB_REF automatically and supports file attachment.
Set env: GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} at the job level, not step level.
Node version: 22 (aligns with stack defaults; satisfies engines.node >=18).
The workflow_dispatch trigger enables manual releases (e.g., hotfixes where tag was already pushed but workflow failed).
Do not use continue-on-error: true anywhere — every step must be a hard gate.
